methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $539M - $10.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Colossal Biosciences
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $10.32B

private.name: New Limit
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $3.1B

data.narrative:
DNA Script, a Series C-stage biotech company that has raised $200M+, operates within a competitive Biotech & Health sector encompassing 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Colossal Biosciences, New Limit, and Chai Discovery.

Two valuation methods apply to this target. Stage-progression analysis, using the $200M raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier, yields $340M. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Colossal Biosciences ($10.3B, Series C), Chai Discovery ($3.8B, Series B), New Limit ($3.1B, Series C), and Eikon Therapeutics ($539M, Series B)—produces a median valuation of $3.5B, with a range of $539M to $10.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied; the sector's P/S median stands at 6.9x across three tickers, but DNA Script's revenue is not available.

The triangulated analysis across both applicable methods supports a valuation range of $289M (low) to $4.0B (high), with a median of $1.9B. The wide spread reflects substantial dispersion within the peer set, particularly the $10.3B outlier (Colossal), and heightened uncertainty around DNA Script's relative technology maturity, pipeline advancement, and capital efficiency versus named comparables. Stage-progression suggests a more conservative floor, while peer medians anchor the mid-to-upper range.
